text: Re: the recent cap on server size and number of players: Both limits will be enforced, therefore servers will match-make in 6 crews of different sizes in order to meet both 6 Crew and 16 Player Limits currently.

Custom servers are still unaffected by today's change. This change has been done due to the reports of crashing and performance issues etc since the update and we want to monitor it over the weekend. (edited)

We are aware of the issues around sword/ADS etc. We would never sneak in a change to gameplay and combat without addressing it so please be reassured that all feedback relating to this is already in the right hands.
@Partners The FAQ around The Arena is now LIVE and can be found here: https://www.seaofthieves.com/community/forums/topic/144709/saying-goodbye-to-the-arena/3
The Midnight Prowl isn't part of the celebration plan, but we've not forgotten about it - it won't be the last that you hear of it.

We've heard and discussed your feedback on the awarding of The Good Boy ship cosmetics to players who had already reached rank 50 with the Sea Dogs, but the thinking behind this gift remains unchanged and it is not a decision we will be reversing. All players who spent enough time in The Arena to reach rank 5 during its lifespan will receive five sets of themed ship cosmetics for free to show our thanks for taking part. This bonus gift is in recognition of those truly dedicated players who spent significant time in The Arena during its almost three-year lifespan, something we feel is worth an additional thank you; it should not be seen as a time-limited reward that players were recently encouraged to grind for and then unfairly prevented from acquiring, hence the date being capped when we announced it.

Just reposting so it doesn't get lost: Seeing some confusion around the Rune Tablets from streams this afternoon, this might be coming from the adventure flow from an old Insiders build. For clarity, the Rune Tablets are a chance drop from the Reaper Ambushes from lighting braziers and are a low frequency drop chance. These Rune Tablets however are not critical to the Adventure and are an extra treasure item when traded to Belle. In a previous Insiders update these were dropped from the Soulflame Captain encounter and were critical to progressing the Adventure - but this was changed and is not the case on Launch today! Progressing the Adventure requires players to follow the Map dropped by the Soulflame Captain.

We have taken on board the sentiment from both here and the community since we gifted The Good Boy ship set to all Partners - thanks for taking the time to provide us with your own and your communities' feedback on this. In line with what we've heard from you and our wider playerbase, we want to advise that going forward cosmetics will only be gifted to your accounts as part of the advanced Twitch Drops programme, alongside the Ancient Coins we add to your account on a monthly basis. As part of this, starting with Season Six, you might've noticed that we have changed this process slightly so you get an automatic entitlement to the Seasonal Plunder Pass rather than you having to purchase it through the front end yourselves. This means that items not already earned in game or via promotions will not be added to any Partner's account, and bonuses or cosmetic requests outside of those listed above will not be considered.

Having judged the wider community sentiment and spoken to Andrew and Mike, we will not be making any changes to the supplies on forts. Looking at it from ALL players perspectives, the supplies on a fort allow players to engage with Sea Forts quickly and easily, which is their intent. There's a decent advantage for putting in the time to sail there, both in terms of food and resources. It allows players to stock up for PvP or to have an enjoyable short session without worrying about supplies, shooting off at islands and wasting time. The vast majority of sentiment around the supplies is positive so although we will continue to monitor it, we will not be making any changes at this time. (edited)

@Partners Not sure how many of you are aware of this but if you get hit on Discord by dodgy DMs, scams etc, you can track them down and report them more easily using their UID - this is their unique Discord ID and makes them easier to search for and report, especially if they are using fancy fonts in their nickname and username that are not on a standard keyboard. To be able to find their UID you need to go to your Settings - Advanced and toggle on Developer mode. This allows you to right click users and 'Copy ID' which gives you their Discord UID. This makes it a lot easier to moderate your spaces.

@Partners For visibility and to prevent the same issues being reported here over and over again I have raised the following this morning: If players encounter a server merge while doing the adventure they lose all progress. This also seems to apply to the new Voyage. G5 commendation (True Legend) tracks players on ship for times completed DO NOT SHOWCASE THIS ON STREAM, IT IS BYPASSING THE INTENDED COMMENDATIONS. If there are more than 5 players in the ritual, any over the 5 may not receive the deed. Players who start the Legend of the Veil can cancel at any time, removing the tornado in the sky. Also a reminder to please raise bug through Support so they can be accurately logged ad we can assess player impact and how widespread it is which helps us prioritise: https://support.seaofthieves.com/hc//requests/new (edited)

Morning @Partners @Affiliate Just responding to some questions we've seen on Season 8 rules over the weekend! There is the chance that crews who dive for a battle can return to their original server to fight a rival crew When hunting another crew, Servers can expand temporarily to 6 crews for the battle and then return to 5 after the losing ship is moved away. We are continuing to monitor the server performance improvements released in Season 8 (along with the temporary expanding size). Alongside further improvements incoming, we are now building a plan to slowly increase player counts (therefore increasing the combination of ships on a server) before getting comfortable on our performance prior to any global ship cap increase. Thanks for all the continued feedback and bug reports coming through, these are being actively triaged and whittled down by the team - will share more on our release plans for these fixes soon!

Hey @Partners and @Affiliate , We have been keeping a close eye on your feedback and questions regarding Season 8. While everyone seems to have fun with the new PVP system, some of the issues also surfaced quite quickly after the launch. I'm going to try and answer some of the most burning questions and highlight the steps we are taking to address these problems: Matchmaking: We are improving matchmaking system in general based on the data we've gathered, and also looking into a possibility to allow crews from the same factions queue up against each other. This should widen player pools and improve matchmaking times. The improvements won't happen immediately, but we are aiming at implementing them in January. The system we have in place for SBMM is also being closely monitored. While it works as intended in most cases, if there aren't any perfect matches waiting for your crew when you opt into your next battle, then we do broaden the net of players you could play with over time to ensure you'll always find a match. Rep farming by losing and griefing: We are looking into ways to identify problematic repeated behavior and certain patterns, such as rep grind by losses. While we can't force people to actively engage in a fight or not in an open world sandbox game, where you rely on tools rather than rules, we are thinking of ways to discourage this type of behavior. How exactly? Too early to tell at the moment. For now we are focused on gathering information and thinking of solutions that feel right for everyone. We feel the pain and frustration of PVP-focused players and creators when they get matched with the crew that intentionally sinks within seconds. And we are kindly asking you to try and stay above the toxicity even in such cases. Please be patient and show good sport in both your wins and losses no matter how these were achieved. While we figure out the technical and engineering solutions, something you can help with is encouraging and promoting good-willed competition and PVP etiquette using your platform. If you encounter toxicity or griefing, as always, we're urge you to report the players by raising a ticket - https://support.seaofthieves.com/hc/en-gb/articles/360016890560 Alliances: Since Season 8 is being based in Adventure mode, there will always be a chance of a third-party joining the dance. And this is all supposed to be part of fun and chaotic spirit of Piracy. However, we recognize there is a huge difference between a couple of ships forming an alliance spontaneously/naturally to help each other fight or complete objectives short term, and Alliances that form with a sole purpose of grinding rep with little effort, gaining an unfair advantage and safety in big numbers. Alliance Servers are... tricky and keep being a bit of a headache from our side, since there is no simple change that would magically fix the whole Alliance system and protect it from any exploits whatsoever. Especially if you consider that Sea of Thieves is largely based around player interaction in the first place. We are, again, monitoring the activity and having wider conversations with different teams at the studio. If you encounter players that are abusing the alliance system to gain advantage in PVP - please record and report these cases to us. Gathering more information on this issue is valuable at this moment. Blunderbombs: Changes to the blunderbombs are coming live soon to help ease the frustrations and stop endless spam. Hope this post helps and provides some clarity from our side. We will keep you updated on the progress. Happy sailing, everyone!

Regarding the Valiant Corsair (Oreo) Set https://news.xbox.com/en-us/2023/01/09/oreo-xbox-prizes-halo-forza-sea-of-thieves/ https://www.theverge.com/2023/1/9/23546167/oreo-microsoft-xbox-cookies-forza-halo-infinite-sea-of-thieves A new collaboration between Xbox and Oreo, bringing in 'Xbox Branded Oreos' to shops in Europe, starting the 16th of January. (Please note, it has been released to some locations already. And while it may be available in your country, that does not mean the website is yet available for you) How to redeem: • Inside a packet are cookies with various patterns on them, themed after controller buttons. These buttons are your initial required code. • Visit https://oreo.eu/ and enter your cookie combination to continue. • Enter your email. • In your email inbox will be your codes for redeeming the Valiant Dawn ship set. A code for Steam and a code for Xbox/MS. • Visit https://account.microsoft.com/billing/redeem (for Xbox/MS) or the 'Activate a Product on Steam...' from the 'Games' button at the top of your Steam window.

We categorise Exploits as something that is detrimental to the player experience or against the Spirit of the Design or damaging to the health of the overall game. While some Exploits have become Tricks - Sword Lunge - because we feel that they are not doing the above and are an acceptable Mechanic for higher end play. The line is always a little blurred to the average players, and even to you guys, but when things get reported we can asses them and act accordingly our add it to our list - Turkey Walling.

@Partners @Partner Legends @Affiliate To put a stop to this - the accounts were banned and are banned every time they are reported. The players in questions are transferring their old GTs to new accounts - which is something allowed at a Microsoft level. The accounts reported were banned. Also, GTs can only be 'spoofed' client side but not for everyone on the server -that data is encrypted server side. All of this 'apparently' and fearmongering from people who have no idea what is and isn't possible, just feeds a dangerous narrative so please be careful of engaging in it. Also, there is no 'code injector' to bypass a redbeard.
Helpful link might be helpful: https://news.xbox.com/en-us/2023/09/21/sea-of-thieves-season-10/

Re: the confusion with flags and Sovereigns: When the Sovereigns were accepting Flags previously, it was not intended. Although this inadvertently added some convenience for cashing into Athena’s, it was not an intended effect and as such there were issues that came alongside this because it was a bug and not as designed. As an example, you would earn Servants of the Flame Allegiance when cashing in Flags at the Sovereign, despite it never being intended as a Reaper’s cash in location. We’ve now fixed the situation to how it was originally intended, that you cannot cash in Flags at the Sovereigns. However, as always intended Guilds adds this convenience as part of being a Guild Emissary. When you are a Guild Emissary, Non-Battle Flags (e.g. a Gold Hoarders Flag) will always be sold to the Reaper’s through the Sovereign. When you are a Guild Emissary Battle Flags (Reaper’s Bones Flags, Athenas Fortune Flag Won in Battle), have now been implemented with the proper logic to ensure your Flags go to the right place. When using a Guild Emissary, the Sovereigns will send Battle Flags to the Reaper’s Bones if you have no faction or are aligned to the Servants, and to Athena’s Fortune if you are aligned to Guardians. They will also send other Trading Company Flags to the Reapers. We also award Athena’s Fortune Reputation if anyone on your crew has bought an Athena’s Fortune Emissary Flag – this is because you only earn Athena’s Fortune Reputation for selling Flags if you are an Athena’s Fortune Emissary – we ensure you don’t miss out on this if you are a Guild Emissary. We also ensure that if you are a Guild Emissary you don’t miss out on the Reputation you would earn as a Reaper or Athena’s Emissary when winning a battle and cashing in the Hourglass. If anyone on your crew has bought an Athena’s Fortune Emissary Flag, when fighting for Guardians you will still get Athena’s Fortune Reputation for Wins and selling the Hourglass. If anyone on your crew has bought a Reaper’s Bones Emissary Flag, when fighting for Servants you will still get Reaper's Bones Reputation for Wins and selling the Hourglass.

@Partners @Partner Legends @Affiliate After the weekend's observations we just wanted to provide an update that there are multiple things lined up to be fixed re: SOSS. ### As always this is NDA The following are being investigated and are scheduled to be fixed but there are no current timelines: Bypassing the speed curse using rowboats Voyage items becoming unusable Abuse of Tall Tale to hide/lose/break items Multiple 'Quests added!' toasts Player can see 'opt out' on other player's masts Galleon and Brig Cursed speed reductions We have also heard the chatter around ships being able to bypass the Voyage by scuttling to get closer to the island and we are investigating game design and mechanics before assessing our next steps. Finally, a small reminder that no one should be abusing mechanics on stream and that if a lot of you are finding these when an update goes live you should really be finding them on Insiders - the amount of noise on some of these from in here over the weekend implies that that is not happening. We encourage you to play Insiders in your ultimate goblin mode and go full degen to find these exploits and help us before release.
Players are unable to join SOSS after completing a round of Hourglass - being fixed but no timelines.
We are TENTATIVELY investigating adding Guild Emmy to SOSS - no timeline and no promises but apparently these conversations were already happening internally. So you can shush up about it now, OK.

@Partner Legends @Partners @Affiliate THE FOLLOWING IS NDA UNTIL THE ACCOMPANYING 'STATE OF PLAY' ARTICLE COMES OUT *We are working on an article that we will put on Forums, Main Twitter and Discord. Once it is ready and live I will also pop it here for you lot to share. * We have put a mitigation in for Guilds that you should be seeing in game now. 1) Infinite spinners on Guilds should have gone away. 2) You will see all the ships you have pledged to your Guild 3) You will also see the ships pledged by one other member (the one besides you, that has pledged the most ships). 4) Some ships may appear missing to you, but they are still pledged the guild. 5) Captained ships you own but aren't pledged to a Guild are unaffected, and you can play then normally. 6) You may not see ships pledged to your guild that are out to sea. You can still join them with normal game invites. In the meantime feel free to just go 'guess they fixed it' and play dumb. Thanks

@Partners @Partner Legends @Affiliate We've noticed that some players are enjoying a particular visual bug that allows them to temporarily have a pirate's cosmetics equipped alongside a Skeleton Curse. While this isn't an intentional feature, it holds no competitive advantage - so for now, we're happy to let folks have some fun with it. However, if we start to see the visual integrity of Sea of Thieves compromised a little too much with the use of this bug, we may take action in future to address this. Feel free to share this in your own words. TLDR - if it gets too jank we will step in sooner, until then, enjoy your screenshots. And no, I have nothing to share re: making this possible or permanent so (edited)

We've received multiple reports that choosing to sail to the Voyage will see the Sea Fort only offering its standard amount of loot. We are updating the text in game and have added front end messaging to the game until the end of the day until these text changes take place. Please advise community members to contact support if they have sailed to their voyages.

Hey @Partners @Partner Legends @Affiliate - Just firing an update here on the topic of creator targeting as I know some of you have been affected by bad actors appearing to lag out servers on-demand recently. We've identified a route where this could be triggered and have initial protections for this arriving in February's update. We suspect this to be a bit of a back and forth though so will continue to monitor this malicious behaviour following the update and work on further protections as we identify them. In order to help our investigations, if you experience further instances following February's update where you feel confident that a bad actor is causing your server to tank (slowly rising Ping and then intense rubberbanding with stuck high ping) then please drop Clips-Gamertags-Timestamps into #exploits-and-unintended-mechanics for us to investigate.

@Partners @Partner Legends @Affiliate Using Crosshair Overlays While the use of Third-Party Tools is against the Sea of Thieves Code of Conduct, the use of Software, Monitor features or Physical solutions to overlay a Crosshair on the screen will not be a bannable offence if used for Sea of Thieves. Sea of Thieves, however, does utilise an Anti-Cheat solution alongside other security tools to protect players from malicious users. Therefore, if an external solution providing Crosshair tools does become flagged by one of these security systems, players may find themselves under enforcement for its use. The use of Software, Monitor features or Physical solutions to provide a Crosshair Overlay is therefore fully at your own risk. Tampering with Game Files Tampering with game files is against the Sea of Thieves Code of Conduct, this includes but is not limited to manipulating settings found within the installation folder or adding additional files with the intent to modify the experience. However, it is not a bannable offence for players to use tools to control their graphics driver settings, such as NVIDIA Control Panel, AMD software or Intel Graphics Software. Using ReShade Filters The use of Third-Party Tools to modify the visual experience of Sea of Thieves such as ReShade filters is considered a bannable offence. However, it is not a bannable offence for players to use tools to control their graphics driver settings, such as NVIDIA Control Panel, AMD software or Intel Graphics Software. Website article: https://support.seaofthieves.com/articles/24643308439314-Clarification-regarding-specific-Enforcement-Policies

@Partners @Partner Legends @Affiliate Morning all, Aware there has been some silence the last week or so, so apologies for that. We did communicate initial steps the day after this was raised but absolutely take on board that there should have been further communication over the last week and we will learn from that. The team are working across three areas here to mitigate the impact from this issue; 1 - Maintaining game stability, the team are working to better handle the large amounts of incoming invites and to remain stable. 2 - In-Game settings, we're assessing what finer-grained options we can provide here to allow players to disable notifications in-game rather than using platform privacy settings. 3 - Working with Platform, we're engaging with the platform team to assess how this type of issue can be addressed at a much deeper level. This work will take a little more time as it is complicated, so please be patient, but it is high priority that we protect you and others affected by these bad actors and we understand your frustration while this is going on. As soon as we can provide a timeline we will but it’s looking very positive from the conversations taking place this morning. I can not provide further details at this time. *In the mean time, you can take the following steps to prevent excessive invites which risk a game crash: * Step 1: Go to https://www.xbox.com/en-US/user/settings/privacy-and-safety Step 2: Under the Others can: section, set the 3rd option to Friends or Blocked (whatever your preference) <---If you have this set to Anyone, they can still get you. Step 3: Open up GameBar with WinKey+G and log out of your account (NOTE: You don't have to uninstall GameBar), Step 4: Ensure that the XBox App is not running in the background. You can end this task in the Task Manager, Step 5: In the in-game Sea of Thieves Settings, go into HUD and turn off Invite Notifications. Step 6: Scramble Gamertags in-game as well Please note, to add further protection you should hide the load-in screen as these are not scrambeld.

@Partner Legends @Partners @Affiliate Just an update before the weekend re: invite spam. As always, this is confidential and not to leave this space. We have a fix for the game crash problems, this will be integrated into next week's monthly update during downtime. Conversations at a platform level around a wider solution for invite spam are still ongoing so I have no further updates right now.

Hey @Partners @Partner Legends @Affiliate , just jumping in here as a follow up to Jo's message above. In today's update 3.4.1 there's two changes that's been made to address the recent Game Invite Spam issues; Stability improvements when receiving large amounts of invites Improvements to the existing 'Disable Invite Notifications' setting to also hide these during the Front End Menu While we continue to work with the platform team to address the root cause of this, hopefully the above two issues begin to mitigate the impact of this on your experience!
